perturbation.original_text: The initial production period shall be for a period of two (2) months to commence on November 4, 1998 (the "Production Period"), and the remaining term of this Agreement shall be for a period of twenty four (24) months to commence on the tentative launch date of January 4, 1999, unless terminated earlier as provided herein (the "Promotion Period"), (The Production Period and the Promotion Period shall be collectively referred to as the "Initial Term"). The Parties agree that prior to July 1, 2000, iVillage will provide FMM with the opportunity to renew this Agreement (the "Renewal Term") on terms set forth in a proposal (the "Proposal) to be presented to FMM. FMM shall indicate its acceptance or rejection of the Proposal no later than August 31, 2000. If iVillage does not receive FMM's acceptance or rejection of the Proposal by August 31, 2000, iVillage may interpret FMM's non response as a rejection of the Proposal. The Proposal shall include maximum payment fees by FMM with respect to the Renewal Term.
perturbation.changed_text: The initial production period shall be for a period of two (2) months to commence on November 4, 1998 (the "Production Period"), and the remaining term of this Agreement shall be for a period of twenty four (24) months to commence on the tentative launch date of January 4, 1999, unless terminated earlier as provided herein (the "Promotion Period"), (The Production Period and the Promotion Period shall be collectively referred to as the "Initial Term"). The Parties agree that prior to July 1, 2000, iVillage may provide FMM with a *recommendation* to renew this Agreement (the "Renewal Term") on terms set forth in a proposal (the "Proposal) to be presented to FMM. FMM shall indicate its acceptance or rejection of the Proposal no later than August 31, 2000. If iVillage does not receive FMM's acceptance or rejection of the Proposal by August 31, 2000, iVillage may interpret FMM's non response as a rejection of the Proposal. The Proposal shall include maximum payment fees by FMM with respect to the Renewal Term.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'opportunity to renew' to 'recommendation to renew' introduces ambiguity. The original text implies a contractual obligation for iVillage to offer a renewal opportunity. The modified text makes it seem like iVillage is merely suggesting a renewal, removing the element of a guaranteed opportunity. This contradicts the section because now the right to renewal depends on what can be argued as a mere suggestion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. The initial production period shall be for a period of two (2) months to commence on November 4, 1998 (the "Production Period"), and the remaining term of this Agreement shall be for a period of twenty four (24) months to commence on the tentative launch date of January 4, 1999, unless terminated earlier as provided herein (the "Promotion Period"), (The Production Period and the Promotion Period shall be collectively referred to as the "Initial Term"). The Parties agree that prior to July 1, 2000, iVillage may provide FMM with a *recommendation* to renew this Agreement (the "Renewal Term") on terms set forth in a proposal (the "Proposal) to be presented to FMM. FMM shall indicate its acceptance or rejection of the Proposal no later than August 31, 2000. If iVillage does not receive FMM's acceptance or rejection of the Proposal by August 31, 2000, iVillage may interpret FMM's non response as a rejection of the Proposal. The Proposal shall include maximum payment fees by FMM with respect to the Renewal Term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'opportunity to renew' to 'recommendation to renew', which fundamentally shifts iVillage's obligation. The original text implies a guaranteed right to renew, whereas the altered text suggests it's merely a suggestion. This contradicts the Contradicted Text, which is identical to the Changed Text, because the renewal is now contingent on a 'recommendation' rather than an opportunity, creating a direct conflict within the agreement concerning renewal terms, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For the Initial Term of this Agreement, iVillage agrees that Ford shall be the exclusive automobile manufacturer sponsor and advertiser throughout the Network, with respect to entities whose primary business is that of an automotive manufacturer and/or retailer. For purposes of this Agreement, the term "retailer" shall refer to an entity which sells new and/or used vehicles. In addition, in the event that iVillage desires to form a sponsorship relationship with an automobile rental company during the term of this Agreement, iVillage shall notify Hertz and provide Hertz with an opportunity to enter into such a relationship with iVillage, on not less favorable terms than those offered to any other automobile rental company. Once presented with an opportunity, Hertz shall have five (5) business days in which to accept or reject such terms. If iVillage does not receive Hertz's acceptance or rejection of such within the allotted time, iVillage shall deem Hertz's silence as rejection. The terms of any such relationship shall be mutually determined by the Parties. Notwithstanding the foregoing, FMC shall, on a non-exclusive basis, 3 be permitted to offer Ford Motor Credit car financing products related to the purchase of Ford vehicles.
perturbation.changed_text: For the Initial Term of this Agreement, iVillage agrees that Ford shall be the exclusive automobile manufacturer sponsor and advertiser throughout the Network, except for instances that are mutually agreed upon by both parties. For purposes of this Agreement, the term "retailer" shall refer to an entity which sells new and/or used vehicles. In addition, in the event that iVillage desires to form a sponsorship relationship with an automobile rental company during the term of this Agreement, iVillage shall notify Hertz and provide Hertz with an opportunity to enter into such a relationship with iVillage, on not less favorable terms than those offered to any other automobile rental company. Once presented with an opportunity, Hertz shall have five (5) business days in which to accept or reject such terms. If iVillage does not receive Hertz's acceptance or rejection of such within the allotted time, iVillage shall deem Hertz's silence as rejection. The terms of any such relationship shall be mutually determined by the Parties. Notwithstanding the foregoing, FMC shall, on a non-exclusive basis, 3 be permitted to offer Ford Motor Credit car financing products related to the purchase of Ford vehicles.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'except for instances that are mutually agreed upon by both parties' to the exclusivity clause undermines Ford's exclusive rights. While the original text guarantees Ford's exclusivity as an automobile manufacturer, the added phrase allows iVillage to potentially partner with other automotive manufacturers or retailers if Ford agrees. This creates an in-text contradiction as the exclusivity is no longer absolute and becomes subject to negotiation, thus creating uncertainty about Ford’s competitive advantage. The contradiction is made more confusing when taking into consideration the text immediately following, which still tries to uphold the importance of an exclusivity arrangement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the Initial Term of this Agreement, iVillage agrees that Ford shall be the exclusive automobile manufacturer sponsor and advertiser throughout the Network, except for instances that are mutually agreed upon by both parties. For purposes of this Agreement, the term "retailer" shall refer to an entity which sells new and/or used vehicles. In addition, in the event that iVillage desires to form a sponsorship relationship with an automobile rental company during the term of this Agreement, iVillage shall notify Hertz and provide Hertz with an opportunity to enter into such a relationship with iVillage, on not less favorable terms than those offered to any other automobile rental company. Once presented with an opportunity, Hertz shall have five (5) business days in which to accept or reject such terms. If iVillage does not receive Hertz's acceptance or rejection of such within the allotted time, iVillage shall deem Hertz's silence as rejection. The terms of any such relationship shall be mutually determined by the Parties. Notwithstanding the foregoing, FMC shall, on a non-exclusive basis, 3 be permitted to offer Ford Motor Credit car financing products related to the purchase of Ford vehicles.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant contradiction. The original text grants Ford an 'exclusive' sponsorship and advertising right. The changed text then qualifies this exclusivity by adding 'except for instances that are mutually agreed upon by both parties'. This addition directly undermines the concept of exclusivity as it allows iVillage to partner with other automotive manufacturers with Ford's consent. The contradicted text, which is the changed text itself, now includes a clause that negates the initial claim of exclusivity. This alteration creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the extent of Ford's exclusivity, impacting Ford's competitive advantage and creating uncertainty in the contract, and adheres to the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.
